title: A Rising Multiple-Dose Tolerability, Safety, and Pharmacokinetic Study of Intravenous Tildrakizumab in Patients With Psoriasis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"he0wq","text":"Psoriasis is a chronic inflammatory skin disorder that affects approximately 2–3% of the population worldwide and has severe effects on patients’ physical and psychological well-being1–3. The discovery that psoriasis is an immune-mediated disease has led to more targeted, effective therapies; recent advances have focused on the interleukin (IL)-12/23p40 subunit shared by IL-12 and IL-23. Evidence suggests that specific inhibition of IL-23 would result in improvement in psoriasis. Here we evaluate tildrakizumab, a monoclonal antibody that targets the IL-23p19 subunit, in a three-part, randomized, placebo-controlled, sequential, rising multiple-dose phase I study in patients with moderate-to-severe psoriasis to provide clinical proof that specific targeting of IL-23p19 results in symptomatic improvement of disease severity in human subjects.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Epub 2015 Mar 9.)\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qpebx","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ymlv","text":"\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"lmqt1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hzcjs","text":"This study evaluates tildrakizumab, a monoclonal antibody that targets the IL-23p19 subunit, in a three-part, randomized, placebo-controlled, sequential, rising multiple-dose phase I study in patients with moderate-to-severe psoriasis to provide clinical proof that specific targeting of IL-23p19 results in symptomatic improvement of disease severity in human subjects.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"tpxpa","text":"Reagents","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"q29p9","text":"This was a clinical study with human subjects receiving tildrakizumab 100 mg/mL injectable intravenous (IV) solution or placebo (sterile water for injection placebo added to 0.9% Saline for IV infusion).\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"h2wia","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ozrr8","text":"\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hcrd5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bclyn","text":"See attached protocol for further details.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"srp4r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dgj4p","text":"Equipment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"s27si","text":"See attached protocol for full details.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Baseline assessments are taken the evening before dosing
Subjects are given study medication after an overnight fast and 2 hours before their first meal.
Part 1 includes 4 dose cohorts: cohorts 1 and 2 consist of 4 subjects each, whereas Cohorts 3 and 4 will consist of 8 subjects each.
Subjects are randomized at a 3 (active) to 1 (placebo) ratio.

Each subject receives a single dose of either 0.1 mg/kg (Cohort 1), 0.5 mg/kg (Cohort 2), 3 mg/kg (Cohort 3) or 10 mg/kg (Cohort 4) of tildrakizumab IV administered over 1 hour; a minimum of 48 hours will elapse between drug administration to subjects.
After an 8-week safety observation period, each subject in Part 1 (Cohorts 1 through 4) receives an additional two doses of tildrakizumab IV at 0.1, 0.5, 3, and 10 mg/kg, respectively, 4 weeks apart.
On Day 2, safety assessments are performed and PK samples obtained.
Subjects receive Dose 2 on Day 56
Subjects receive Dose 3 on Day 84
Subjects have a closeout visit within 14 days of the last safety and PK assessment.
Up to 40 subjects with psoriasis are enrolled in Part 2
Subjects are put into 2 dose cohorts (Cohorts 5 and 6).
Subjects in Cohort 5 receive 3 doses of 3 mg/kg tildrakizumab IV given at Week 0, 4, and 8.
Subjects in Cohort 6 receive 3 doses of 10 mg/kg tildrakizumab IV given at Week 0, 4, and 8.
Subjects are randomized in a 3:1 ratio of active to placebo.
Serum pharmacokinetic samples are obtained predose and at the intervals specified in the attached full protocol
Subjects receive Dose 2 and 3 of tildrakizumab during their visits on Days 28 and 56.
Subjects have a closeout visit within 14 days of the last PK assessment.
A closeout visit will be scheduled within 14 days of the last PK assessment.

The primary objectives of this study are to determine the safety, tolerability, and pharmacokinetics of rising intravenous doses of tildrakizumab in subjects with moderate to severe psoriasis. The secondary objectives of this study are to assess the effect of tildrakizumab on skin disease in subjects with moderate to severe psoriasis; and to assess the effects of tildrakizumab on biomarkers of the IL-23 pathway and related immune phenomena.

T.K., H.K. and T.M. receive research support from Merck & Co., Inc. Clinical improvement in psoriasis with specific targeting of interleukin-23 by Tamara Kopp, Elisabeth Riedl, Christine Bangert, Edward P. Bowman, Elli Greisenegger, Ann Horowitz, Harald Kittler, Wendy M. Blumenschein, Terrill K. McClanahan, Thomas Marbury, Claus Zachariae, Danlin Xu, Xiaoli Shirley Hou, Anish Mehta, Anthe S. Zandvli published in Nature on 09 Mar, 2015 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nature14175
